# arb4j — Copilot Instructions

arb4j is a Java API wrapping arblib/FLINT (arbitrary-precision ball arithmetic) via SWIG-generated JNI, plus an expression compiler that turns Unicode mathematical notation directly into JVM bytecode via ASM.

## Build & Test

```bash
# Build (the committed libarblib.so is already current — no FLINT build needed)
JAVA_HOME=/usr/lib/jvm/java-26-openjdk-amd64 \
LANG=en_US.UTF-8 LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8 \
mvn install -Dmaven.test.skip=true

# Run all tests
mvn test

# Run a single test class or method
mvn test -Dtest=RealTest#testSqrt
mvn test -Dtest='RationalFunctionTest#testPowers'

# Run with expression-compiler tracing
mvn -Ptrace test -Dtest=ClassName#methodName
# Equivalent: -Darb4j.trace=true -Darb4j.traceNodes=true -Dlogback.configurationFile=logback-debug.xml

# Rebuild native lib — ONLY needed when SWIG interface files (native/*.i) change
make && mvn install -Dmaven.test.skip=true
```

**Mandatory environment:** Java 26 (`--enable-preview`), `LANG=en_US.UTF-8 LC_ALL=en_US.UTF-8` (source files have Unicode names: `σField.java`, `RiemannζFunction.java`, etc.).

**`make` is not needed for ordinary Java work.** `libarblib.so` is a prebuilt, committed, statically-linked binary at `src/main/resources/native/libarblib.so` (symlinked to repo root). No system FLINT/MPFR/GMP is required.

## Debugging / Tracing Flags

Set as `-D` system properties or as static fields before compiling an expression:

| Property | Effect |
|---|---|
| `-Darb4j.saveClasses=true` | Write generated `.class` files to `compiled/` |
| `-Darb4j.decompileClasses=true` | Decompile generated classes to `compiled/<name>.java` |
| `-Darb4j.saveGraphs=true` | Dump GraphViz of the AST |
| `-Darb4j.trace=true` | Log every parse/compile step |
| `-Darb4j.traceNodes=true` | Log per-node bytecode emission |

When diagnosing a compiler bug, always read `compiled/<name>.java` or run `javap -p -c compiled/<name>.class`. **Never guess** — there is always a deterministic path to the answer.

## Architecture

### Layer 1 — Native wrappers: `arb.*`
`Real`, `Complex`, `Integer`, `Float`, `Magnitude`, `RealPolynomial`, `ComplexPolynomial`, `ComplexMatrix`, `Fraction`, `RationalFunction` are thin Java holders for arblib native structs.

- **All are `AutoCloseable`** — own native memory; always use try-with-resources or call `.close()`.
- **Fluent last-arg-is-result convention:** `x.sqrt(128)` returns a new `Real`; `x.sqrt(128, result)` writes into `result`. The bytecode emitter targets this shape — changing a method's signature breaks generated code at *runtime*, not compile time.
- **Scalar-or-vector:** `new Real()` is a scalar; `Real.newVector(n)` is a vector. Access via `x.get(i)`, dimension via `x.dim()`.

### Layer 2 — Expression compiler: `arb.expressions.*`
Pipeline: `Expression.parseRoot` (recursive-descent, Unicode-aware) → AST of `Node` subclasses in `arb.expressions.nodes.*` → ASM bytecode emission → `ExpressionClassLoader` → instantiation with injected variable/function references via `Context`.

**Two public APIs for recursive clusters:**
- `parseCompileAndRegister(name, …, expression, …, context)` — define only (bytecode emitted, no instantiation). Use for every member of a mutual-recursion cluster except the last.
- `express(name, …, expression, …, context)` — define + instantiate. Use for the last (or only) member, after all peers are defined.

**Why this matters:** Instantiation calls `Context.injectVariableReferences` / `injectFunctionReferences`, which use `Class.getFields()` to resolve every field's type. If a peer class isn't defined yet in the `ExpressionClassLoader`, this throws `NoClassDefFoundError`. Class *definition* does not trigger resolution, so `parseCompileAndRegister` is safe to call in any order.

### Layer 3 — Hand-written math: `arb.functions.*`
`Function`, `Sequence`, `RealFunction`, `ComplexFunction`, etc., and subpackages by domain (`real/`, `complex/`, `integer/`, `rational/`, `algebraic/`, `polynomials/orthogonal/{real,complex}`). These are the typed interfaces that generated classes implement. The generics on `Expression<D, C, F>` (Domain, Codomain, Function type) are not decorative — they drive bytecode field declarations and method signatures.

### Applications: `arb.applications.*`
JavaFX programs (`Expressor`, `SymbolPalette`, `ReferenceBrowser`, etc.) launched via `bin/run <class>`, `bin/expressor`, `bin/arbshell`, `bin/symbolPalette`. `bin/run` reads `class.path` (regenerated by `mvn generate-sources`) and adds all `--add-modules`/`--add-opens` flags.

## Tests

JUnit 4 under `src/test/java/arb/`, mirroring the main package layout. Tests commonly set `Expression.saveClasses = true` to dump generated bytecode to `compiled/` — preserve this in new compiler tests.

**Never read `build/surefire-reports/` for pass/fail status.** That directory accumulates stale reports from deleted and renamed test classes across all prior runs. Trust only the live `mvn test` console output.

## Hard Rules

### NEVER guess
Words like "probably", "likely", "I bet", "might be", "could be", "suspicion" are banned. Use the tracing flags, decompiler, `javap`, or a debugger to get the actual answer.

### NEVER manipulate expression strings programmatically
Expression strings passed to `*.express(...)`, `*.compile(...)`, `*.declare(...)` must always be string literals. The following are forbidden without exception:
- Concatenation with `+` to splice runtime values into an expression ❌
- `String.format(...)`, `String.replace(...)`, `StringBuilder` on an expression ❌

If a naming collision in a shared `Context` is forcing the temptation — rethink the architecture instead (per-instance `Context`, shared pre-compiled `Sequence` fields, parent-class refactor).

### NEVER use Java arrays of arb types
`Real[]`, `Complex[]`, `Integer[]`, `Magnitude[]` etc. are wrong. These types *are* the vector. Use:
- `Real.newVector(n)` / `Complex.newVector(n)` for vectors
- `x.get(i)` for indexed access, `x.dim()` for dimension

### NEVER write Unicode escape sequences
Always write the literal character: `σ` not `\u03c3`, `➜` not `\u279c`. Source is UTF-8 throughout.

### NEVER use fully-qualified class names inline
Add an `import` and use the simple name. `arb.Integer` inline in code is wrong; `import arb.Integer; ... Integer` is correct.

### NEVER implement "fallback" paths
Pick one algorithm, prove it correct on its full domain, throw on out-of-domain inputs. Silent fallback to a different algorithm is forbidden. The word "fallback" must not appear anywhere.

### NEVER run destructive git commands on uncommitted files
Before any `git checkout <file>`, `git restore`, `git reset --hard`, `git clean -fd`, or `git stash drop`:
1. `git status --short` to check for uncommitted work.
2. If anything is modified/untracked, commit to a WIP branch first.

### The words "body" and "curry" are FORBIDDEN
Use "expression" or "definition" instead of "body". Describe `i➔j➔…` sequences as nested/indexed function sequences, never as "curried".

### Behavior lives on the type — use polymorphism
Each `Node` subclass owns its own `generate`, `differentiate`, `simplify`. Node-producing operations construct the same concrete subclass they were called on. Do not add type-switching `instanceof` chains.
